memes.label: "George Finch"
memes.description: "George Finch, the protagonist of The Small Bachelor, is a young artist living in Greenwich Village who falls in love with Molly Waddington."

memes.label: "Greenwich Village"
memes.description: "Greenwich Village, New York's Bohemian quarter, is the setting for The Small Bachelor and the home of its artist protagonist George Finch."

memes.label: "Hamilton Beamish"
memes.description: "Hamilton Beamish is an efficiency expert and friend to George Finch who falls victim to the very love at first sight he has written a booklet arguing against."

memes.label: "The Small Bachelor · Imprint"
memes.description: "Imprint page of The Small Bachelor"

memes.label: "Molly Waddington"
memes.description: "Molly Waddington is the romantic heroine of The Small Bachelor, the daughter of Mrs. Waddington who defies her mother's social ambitions to marry George Finch."

memes.label: "Mrs. Waddington"
memes.description: "Mrs. Waddington is the principal antagonist of The Small Bachelor, a socially ambitious woman who opposes her daughter Molly's marriage to George Finch on financial grounds."

memes.label: "The Purple Chicken"
memes.description: "The Purple Chicken is a restaurant in The Small Bachelor that serves as a gathering place for characters and a symbol of Bohemian New York."

memes.label: "Sheridan Apartments"
memes.description: "The Sheridan apartment house in Greenwich Village is the primary setting of The Small Bachelor, home to George Finch and the heart of New York's Bohemian quarter."

memes.label: "Sigsbee H. Waddington"
memes.description: "Sigsbee H. Waddington is Molly's father and Mrs. Waddington's husband, a largely passive figure whose wealth and status define his family's social position."

memes.label: All Topics
memes.description: A complete index of all entries in The Small Bachelor wiki, grouped by type and quality tier.

memes.label: "Art and Artists"
memes.description: "The role and social standing of art and artists in Prohibition-era New York as depicted in The Small Bachelor — George Finch's identity as a painter is the engine of both the novel's comedy and its social critique."

relations.label: Finer and Better Motion Picture Company

memes.label: "Bachelorhood"
memes.description: "The state of bachelorhood as the title theme of The Small Bachelor — George Finch's identity as a 'small bachelor' and what it signifies about independence, class, and the 1920s New York social landscape."

memes.label: "Beamish Booklets"
memes.description: "Hamilton Beamish's self-published pamphlets on efficiency, scientific living, and pure English — a running comic device in The Small Bachelor that represents the gap between abstract theory and lived experience."

relations.label: Finer and Better Motion Picture Company

memes.label: "Beamish Falls in Love on the Bus"
memes.description: "Hamilton Beamish meets an unknown woman on a city bus and falls in love at first sight — the comic reversal that demolishes his own efficiency theories and drives the novel's second romantic plot."

memes.label: "Brangmarley Hall"
memes.description: "Brangmarley Hall, Little-Seeping-in-the-Wold, Salop, England — the butler Ferris's lost paradise and the novel's symbol of an ordered English past."

memes.label: Characters in The Small Bachelor
memes.description: A comprehensive index of all characters appearing in P.G. Wodehouse's The Small Bachelor, sorted alphabetically.

memes.label: "Class and Society"
memes.description: "Social class and respectability as satirical themes in The Small Bachelor, explored through character judgments and social climbing in Prohibition-era New York."

memes.label: Concepts in The Small Bachelor
memes.description: An index of key concepts, themes, and ideas explored in P.G. Wodehouse's The Small Bachelor.

memes.label: "Crime and Law"
memes.description: "Crime, law enforcement, and legal complications in The Small Bachelor, set against the backdrop of Prohibition-era New York."

memes.label: "Deception and Mistaken Identity"
memes.description: "Deception, disguise, and mistaken identity as recurring comic devices in The Small Bachelor — how Wodehouse uses imposture and misrecognition to generate farce and advance the romantic plot."

memes.label: East Gilead
memes.description: George Finch's hometown in Idaho — a "stuffy little hamlet" whose provincial customs and romantic entanglements haunt the protagonist throughout "The Small Bachelor."

memes.label: "Efficiency and Self-Improvement"
memes.description: "The cult of efficiency and self-improvement as embodied by J. Hamilton Beamish in The Small Bachelor — Wodehouse's satire of early-twentieth-century scientific management and the self-help movement."

memes.label: "Engagement and Courtship"
memes.description: "The rituals of courtship, engagement, and romantic pursuit in The Small Bachelor — how Wodehouse uses the conventions of romantic comedy to drive plot and satirize social expectations."

memes.label: "Family and Duty"
memes.description: "Family relationships and the conflict between parental duty and individual happiness in The Small Bachelor."

memes.label: "Fanny Welch"
memes.description: "Fanny Welch is a pickpocket and the fiancée of Mullett in The Small Bachelor. Her criminal past and desire for reform mirror the novel's themes of self-reinvention."

memes.label: "Fashion and Appearance"
memes.description: "Dress, fashion, and personal appearance as markers of social identity and emotional state in The Small Bachelor — how Wodehouse uses clothing to signal class, character, and romantic interest."

memes.label: "Fate and Coincidence"
memes.description: "The role of chance encounters and comic coincidence as plot mechanisms in The Small Bachelor."

memes.label: "Ferris"
memes.description: "Ferris is the butler at the Waddington household in The Small Bachelor. Nostalgic for Brangmarley Hall, he serves as a quiet comic observer of the family's domestic chaos."

memes.label: "Fifth Avenue"
memes.description: "Fifth Avenue as the symbol of wealth and social respectability in The Small Bachelor, in contrast to Greenwich Village."

memes.label: "Finer and Better Motion Picture Company"
memes.description: "A comic business venture in The Small Bachelor that parodies the early film industry — notable primarily for what it lacks: a studio, expensive directors, and costly talent."

memes.label: "Food and Dining"
memes.description: "Food and dining as social rituals and comic settings in The Small Bachelor, including the Purple Chicken restaurant."

memes.label: "Fortune and Inheritance"
memes.description: "Inherited wealth as a plot device in The Small Bachelor — George Finch's fortune from Uncle Thomas and its social consequences."

memes.label: "Gideon Voules"
memes.description: "The Reverend Gideon Voules is the clergyman who officiates at the wedding in The Small Bachelor. His poached-egg expression and white socks make him one of Wodehouse's memorable comic clergymen."

memes.label: "Giuseppe"
memes.description: "Giuseppe is a waiter at the Purple Chicken restaurant in The Small Bachelor whose multilingual encouragement during the tablecloth fracas provides one of the novel's most surreal comic moments."

memes.label: "Great Neck Social and Literary Society"
memes.description: "The Great Neck Social and Literary Society is a Long Island cultural organization in The Small Bachelor at whose dinner Hamilton Beamish is scheduled to speak — providing a key alibi and plot complication."
aliases: Great Neck Social and Literary Society

memes.label: "Humor and Comedy"
memes.description: "The comic techniques and satirical mode of The Small Bachelor, including Wodehouse's use of narrative voice, absurd situations, and social satire."

memes.label: "Lancelot Biffen"
memes.description: "Editor of Town Gossip and resident of the Sheridan Apartments in The Small Bachelor. A 'ghastly, sneaking, snooping' gossip columnist whose professional interest in scandal makes him a minor antagonist."

memes.label: "Long Island"
memes.description: "Long Island — specifically Hempstead — is the location of the Waddington summer residence and the setting for the novel's climactic wedding in The Small Bachelor."

memes.label: Lord Hunstanton
memes.description: British aristocrat and house-guest of the Waddingtons in P.G. Wodehouse's "The Small Bachelor," whose impeccable etiquette and romantic designs on Molly Waddington make him a comic antagonist.

memes.label: "Love and Romance"
memes.description: "The theme of love and romance in The Small Bachelor — centered on the comic tension between love at first sight and the rational efficiency that Hamilton Beamish's booklets champion."

memes.label: "Madame Eulalie"
memes.description: "A fortune-teller in The Small Bachelor whose crystal-ball revelations and friendly disposition drive several key plot complications. Her fortune-telling practice attracts police attention under Prohibition-era laws."

memes.label: "Marriage and Weddings"
memes.description: "Marriage as a plot mechanism and social institution in The Small Bachelor, including the comic obstacles to the wedding of George Finch and Molly Waddington."

memes.label: "May Stubbs"
memes.description: "A figure from George Finch's past in The Small Bachelor whose prior romantic entanglement with George creates one of the novel's central comic complications. Her arrival in New York sets in motion a chain of farcical events."

memes.label: "Money and Wealth"
memes.description: "The role of money and wealth as determinants of social standing and romantic eligibility in The Small Bachelor."

memes.label: Mullett
memes.description: Reformed ex-convict and valet to George Finch in P.G. Wodehouse's "The Small Bachelor," whose criminal past and colorful vocabulary provide comic relief throughout the novel.

memes.label: New York
memes.description: The primary setting of P.G. Wodehouse's "The Small Bachelor" — Prohibition-era Manhattan, where Greenwich Village Bohemianism collides with Fifth Avenue society.

memes.label: "Officer Garroway"
memes.description: "A New York police officer in The Small Bachelor who, under Hamilton Beamish's tutelage, aspires to become a poet. His hay fever, gallantry, and improbable literary ambitions make him one of Wodehouse's most endearing comic creations."

memes.label: Places in The Small Bachelor
memes.description: A comprehensive index of all locations and places appearing in P.G. Wodehouse's The Small Bachelor.

memes.label: "Police and Law"
memes.description: "Police and law enforcement as a comic force in The Small Bachelor, centred on Officer Garroway and the Mullett subplot."

memes.label: "Prohibition"
memes.description: "Prohibition — the legal banning of alcohol in 1920s America — provides the backdrop for The Small Bachelor. Wodehouse treats the era's absurdities with characteristic light satire."

memes.label: "Servants and Domestic Life"
memes.description: "The role of servants and domestic life in The Small Bachelor — how Wodehouse uses the servant class as comic observers and unlikely agents of plot resolution."

memes.label: "The Beamish Booklets"
memes.description: "The self-improvement booklets authored by J. Hamilton Beamish, a central satirical device in The Small Bachelor that lampoons the early-twentieth-century self-help craze and the cult of efficiency."

memes.label: "United Beef"
memes.description: "United Beef is a wealthy business magnate in The Small Bachelor whose casual revelation about the Finer and Better Film Company's success drives a key comic reversal."

relations.label: Finer and Better Motion Picture Company

memes.label: "The Waddington Dinner Party"
memes.description: "The formal dinner party at the Waddington residence — a pivotal early scene in The Small Bachelor where George Finch's social standing is tested against the expectations of Fifth Avenue society."

memes.label: "Washington Square"
memes.description: "Washington Square as the geographic and social anchor of The Small Bachelor — home to the Sheridan Apartments and the Bohemian world of George Finch."
